vitals.terrain: Mostly flat coastal with wind challenges
vitals.participation_trend: Second edition in 2025; growing GFNY presence in Peru

course_description.overview: 116 km road course starting and finishing at Kite Club Paracas, featuring a route through Paracas National Reserve along Peru's Pacific coast with desert scenery, powerful coastal winds, and moderate elevation.

key_segments.name: Paracas National Reserve
key_segments.description: Route enters the reserve, turning at San Martin port.

key_segments.name: Coastal Winds Section
key_segments.description: Mostly flat but tested by strong Pacific winds sweeping across desert.

key_segments.name: Access Road to Paracas
key_segments.description: Proceeds along access road back to finish.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Challenging due to coastal winds despite mostly flat profile and modest 662 m elevation gain; fast starts with speeds over 50 km/h observed.

logistics.getting_there: 3.5 hours south of Lima by road; start/finish at Kite Club Paracas.
logistics.accommodation: Kite Club Paracas hosts race week activities; explore Paracas National Reserve and Ballestas Islands nearby.
logistics.registration_details: Available via official GFNY Paracas website; part of GFNY Global Endurance Sports Series.
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bikes suitable; group ride on Saturday for casual preview.
logistics.local_considerations: Desert meets Pacific Ocean; pre-Inca archaeological sites and wildlife; expect coastal winds.

final_verdict.one_liner: Wind-swept coastal gran fondo in Peru's stunning Paracas desert.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Road cyclists seeking flat-but-windy endurance challenge with exotic scenery and GFNY qualifier perks.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes for adventure seekers in South America; solid regional GFNY event.
pro_tips: Train for crosswinds on flat roads.
Arrive early from Lima (3.5 hrs drive).
Join Saturday group ride to scout.
